applied_optimizations.name: 2-pass algorithm (sum + sum_sq in single pass)
applied_optimizations.description: Reduced from 3 passes (mean, variance, normalize) to 2 passes by computing sum and sum_of_squares together in the first pass, then deriving mean = sum/N and var = sum_sq/N - mean*mean. This cuts memory traffic by ~33%.

applied_optimizations.name: Simplified 1D indexing
applied_optimizations.description: Used direct 1D offset into the contiguous (H, W) slice since NCHW is contiguous in last 2 dimensions, eliminating expensive division and modulo operations.

attempted_but_failed.name: Welford's algorithm single-pass
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton doesn't support element-by-element indexing within a block using mask[i]

attempted_but_failed.name: Two-kernel approach (stats + apply)
attempted_but_failed.reason: Worse performance due to extra kernel launch overhead

notes: Key optimization: reducing memory passes from 3 to 2
BLOCK_SIZE=65536 works well for 512x512 spatial dimensions
